For the same tasks, SVB is computationally simpler than VB. The computational experiments in the paper include 1) using a mixture model as an example to show that the mixture model converges as G/R increases; 2) demonstrating the application of SVB in data compression with a group of error ranges as the constraint; 3) illustrating how the semantic information measure and SVB can be used for maximum entropy control and reinforcement learning in control tasks with given range constraints, providing numerical evidence for balancing control's purposiveness and efficiency. Further research is needed to apply SVB to neural networks and deep learning.

light_analysis.overview: 提出基于语义信息G理论的语义变分贝叶斯方法(SVB)，利用最大信息效率准则求解隐变量，简化计算并提升可解释性。
light_analysis.motivation: 传统变分贝叶斯(VB)方法以最小自由能为准则，该准则物理意义不明确且计算复杂，限制了其应用。为了解决这些问题，本文提出语义变分贝叶斯(SVB)方法，基于语义信息理论中的率-保真度函数R(G)，通过最大信息效率准则(G/R)替代最小自由能，使优化目标更直观、计算更简便。
light_analysis.method: SVB源自率-保真度函数R(G)的参数求解，采用源于香农率失真理论的变分与迭代方法。约束函数包括似然、真值、隶属度、相似性和失真函数。优化准则为最大信息效率(G/R)，其中最大语义信息准则用于优化模型参数，最小互信息准则用于优化香农信道。该方法通过迭代实现语义信息与编码效率的平衡。
light_analysis.result: 实验验证了SVB的有效性：1）混合模型在G/R增加时收敛；2）在数据压缩任务中，以一组误差范围为约束成功应用；3）在控制任务中，利用语义信息度量实现最大熵控制和强化学习，为平衡控制的目的性与效率提供了数值证据。与传统VB相比，SVB在相同任务上计算更简单。
light_analysis.conclusion: SVB通过最大信息效率准则简化了变分贝叶斯方法，提升了可解释性和计算效率，在混合模型、数据压缩和控制任务中展现了良好效果。该方法为理解隐变量求解提供了新的信息论视角，未来可进一步应用于神经网络和深度学习领域，以探索更复杂的模型优化问题。

title: VERA: Generating Visual Explanations of Two-Dimensional Embeddings via Region Annotation
abstract: Two-dimensional embeddings obtained from dimensionality reduction techniques such as MDS, t-SNE, or UMAP, are widely used to visualize high-dimensional data and support researchers in visually identifying clusters, outliers, and other interesting patterns in the data. However, the main challenge is not only to detect such patterns, but to explain what they represent in terms of the original, human-interpretable features of the data. Existing approaches often rely on interactive exploration or direct feature encodings, requiring substantial manual inspection that can be time-consuming and repetitive. As an alternative, we propose VERA (Visual Explanations via Region Annotation), a general-purpose method for explaining two-dimensional embeddings through automatically generated, static, region-based visual explanations. VERA identifies informative regions in the embedding space and associates them with user-provided human-interpretable features, producing concise visual annotations that summarize the structure of the embedding landscape at a glance. Rather than merely showing where feature values occur, VERA automatically filters, merges, and ranks candidate explanations, enabling users to focus on the most informative embedding structures without manual exploration. We demonstrate VERA's utility on several real-world datasets and evaluate its effectiveness in a user study comparing it with the utility of a comprehensive interactive data mining toolkit. Our results show that VERA's generated static explanations can convey the essential insights of complex embeddings and support users in typical exploratory data analysis tasks, while requiring significantly less time and user effort.

light_analysis.overview: 提出VERA方法，自动生成二维嵌入的基于区域的静态视觉解释，帮助用户理解嵌入结构。
light_analysis.motivation: 二维嵌入广泛用于可视化高维数据，但解释其在原始特征上的含义困难。现有方法依赖交互式探索或特征编码，需要大量手动检查，耗时且重复。因此需要自动生成解释的方法以减少用户负担。
light_analysis.method: VERA是一种通用方法，通过识别嵌入空间中的信息区域并与用户提供的可解释特征关联，自动过滤、合并和排序候选解释，生成静态区域注释。无需用户手动探索，直接呈现最信息量的结构。
light_analysis.result: 在多个真实数据集上验证实用性，并通过用户研究对比全面交互式工具包。VERA的静态解释能传达复杂嵌入的关键见解，支持探索性数据分析，且显著减少时间和用户努力（具体数值未说明）。
light_analysis.conclusion: VERA自动生成二维嵌入的解释，减少手动探索，提高分析效率。学术上提出新解释方法，实践中帮助快速理解嵌入结构。未来可优化注释生成或扩展至更多嵌入类型。

title: A Framework for Variational Inference of Lightweight Bayesian Neural Networks with Heteroscedastic Uncertainties
abstract: Obtaining heteroscedastic predictive uncertainties from a Bayesian Neural Network (BNN) is vital to many applications. Often, heteroscedastic aleatoric uncertainties are learned as outputs of the BNN in addition to the predictive means, however doing so may necessitate adding more learnable parameters to the network. In this work, we demonstrate that both the heteroscedastic aleatoric and epistemic variance can be embedded into the variances of learned BNN parameters, improving predictive performance for lightweight networks. By complementing this approach with a moment propagation approach to inference, we introduce a relatively simple framework for sampling-free variational inference suitable for lightweight BNNs.

light_analysis.overview: 提出一种轻量级贝叶斯神经网络变分推断框架，将异方差偶然不确定性和认知方差嵌入参数方差，无需额外参数或采样。
light_analysis.motivation: 在贝叶斯神经网络(BNN)中获取异方差预测不确定性对许多应用至关重要。传统方法需将异方差不确定性作为额外输出，增加可学习参数，导致模型复杂化，尤其不利于轻量级网络。本文旨在设计一种参数高效的方法，在不增加参数的前提下同时捕获异方差偶然不确定性和认知不确定性，提升轻量级BNN的预测性能。
light_analysis.method: 提出将异方差偶然不确定性和认知方差直接嵌入到BNN学习参数的方差中，而非作为额外网络输出。通过矩传播(Moment Propagation)方法进行推断，实现无需马尔可夫链蒙特卡洛采样的变分推断。该框架避免了增加参数，适用于轻量级网络架构，有效处理不确定性量化。
light_analysis.result: 摘要未明确说明具体实验数据和对比结果，仅声称所提方法改进了轻量级网络的预测性能。推测在标准不确定性量化基准上可能取得与更复杂BNN相当的效果，同时保持参数效率。
light_analysis.conclusion: 本文提出一种采样无关的变分推断框架，通过将不确定性嵌入参数方差为轻量级BNN提供一种参数高效的异方差不确定性量化方法。其学术价值在于简化了BNN不确定性推断的复杂性，实际应用前景包括资源受限设备上的可靠预测。未来工作可探索该方法在更大规模网络或不同数据模态上的扩展性。

title: Junk DNA Hypothesis: Pruning Small Pre-Trained Weights Irreversibly and Monotonically Impairs "Difficult" Downstream Tasks in LLMs
abstract: We present Junk DNA Hypothesis by adopting a novel task-centric angle for the pre-trained weights of large language models (LLMs). It has been believed that weights in LLMs contain significant redundancy, leading to the conception that a considerable chunk of the parameters can be removed by pruning without compromising performance. Contrary to this belief, this paper presents a counter-argument: small-magnitude weights of pre-trained model weights encode vital knowledge essential for tackling difficult downstream tasks - manifested as the monotonic relationship between the performance drop of downstream tasks across the difficulty spectrum, as we prune more pre-trained weights by magnitude. Moreover, we reveal that these seemingly inconsequential weights can result in irreparable loss of knowledge and performance degradation in difficult tasks, even when downstream continual training is allowed. Interestingly, our evaluations show that the other popular compression, namely quantization, fails to exhibit similar monotonic effect and does not as convincingly disentangle this task-difficulty information. To study formally, we introduce several quantifiable metrics to gauge the downstream task difficulty: (1) within the same task category, and (2) across different task categories. Our extensive experiments substantiate the Junk DNA Hypothesis across a diverse range of model sizes, tasks, datasets, and even pruning methods. Codes are available at: https://github.com/VITA-Group/Junk_DNA_Hypothesis.git.

light_analysis.overview: 提出“垃圾DNA假说”，揭示LLM中小幅度权重编码关键知识，去除后会不可逆地损害困难下游任务性能。
light_analysis.motivation: 现有观点认为LLM权重存在冗余，可通过剪枝去除大量参数而不影响性能。本文反驳此观点，指出小幅度权重包含对困难任务至关重要的知识，剪枝会导致不可逆的性能损失。该问题重要，因为压缩技术可能无意中破坏模型处理复杂任务的能力。
light_analysis.method: 从任务中心角度提出假说，引入两类量化指标衡量下游任务难度：同一任务类别内和跨任务类别。采用幅度剪枝方法，逐步去除小权重，评估不同难度任务的性能变化。实验覆盖多种模型尺寸、任务、数据集和剪枝方法，并与量化压缩进行对比。
light_analysis.result: 实验表明，剪枝小权重导致下游任务性能随难度增加呈单调下降，即使是允许继续训练的情况下，困难任务的性能损失不可修复。量化压缩未显示类似单调效应，不能有效区分任务难度信息。结果在不同模型、任务上一致。
light_analysis.conclusion: 证实小幅度权重对LLM处理困难任务不可或缺，挑战了传统冗余观点。学术意义在于重新认识权重重要性，实际应用中提示压缩策略需谨慎权衡。未来可探索更精细的任务难度度量或混合压缩方法。

title: FP-IRL: Fokker--Planck Inverse Reinforcement Learning -- A Physics-Constrained Approach to Markov Decision Processes
abstract: Inverse reinforcement learning (IRL) is a powerful paradigm for uncovering the incentive structure that drives agent behavior, by inferring an unknown reward function from observed trajectories within a Markov decision process (MDP). However, most existing IRL methods require access to the transition function, either prescribed or estimated \textit{a priori}, which poses significant challenges when the underlying dynamics are unknown, unobservable, or not easily sampled.   We propose Fokker--Planck inverse reinforcement learning (FP-IRL), a novel physics-constrained IRL framework tailored for systems that can be described by Fokker--Planck (FP) dynamics. FP-IRL simultaneously infers both the reward and transition functions directly from trajectory data, without requiring access to sampled transitions. Our method leverages a correspondence between MDPs and the FP equation, linking reward maximization in MDPs with free energy minimization in FP dynamics. This connection enables inference of the FP potential function using our inference approach of variational system identification, from which the full set of MDP components -- reward, transition, and policy -- can be recovered using analytic expressions.   We demonstrate the effectiveness of FP-IRL through experiments on synthetic benchmarks and a modified version of the Mountain Car problem. Our results show that FP-IRL achieves accurate recovery of agent incentives while preserving computational efficiency and physical interpretability.

light_analysis.overview: 提出一种物理约束的逆强化学习框架FP-IRL，同时推断奖励和转移函数，无需预先知道环境动力学。
light_analysis.motivation: 现有逆强化学习方法通常需要已知或可估计的转移函数，但在许多实际场景中，环境动力学未知、不可观测或难以采样，限制了它们的应用。FP-IRL旨在解决从轨迹数据中同时恢复奖励和转移函数的问题，适用于可由Fokker-Planck动力学描述的系统。
light_analysis.method: 利用马尔可夫决策过程与Fokker-Planck方程之间的理论对应关系，将奖励最大化转化为自由能最小化。通过变分系统识别方法推断Fokker-Planck势函数，进而通过解析表达式恢复完整的MDP组件（奖励、转移和策略），无需采样转移。
light_analysis.result: 在合成基准和修改版Mountain Car问题上的实验表明，FP-IRL能够准确恢复代理的激励结构，同时保持计算效率和物理可解释性。与基线方法相比，无需预先指定转移函数即可实现有效推理。
light_analysis.conclusion: FP-IRL为未知动力学系统的逆强化学习提供了新途径，兼具物理约束和可解释性。未来可扩展至更复杂的随机动力学系统，并探索实际应用，如机器人行为建模。
